sharedStrengths: Both support the shared hidden .hforge runtime model.
Both support commands, agents, contexts, and maintenance flows.
headlineVerdict: Both are first-class runtime targets, but Claude Code offers stronger native hook/plugin posture.

left.notes: Codex is first-class for maintenance flows.

right.notes: Claude Code is also first-class for maintenance flows.

capabilityComparisons.operatorImpact: Either target can be used confidently for runtime maintenance and health workflows.

left.fallbackBehavior: Use docs and packaged hook manifests, then wire manually.

right.notes: Claude Code offers native hook/plugin posture.

capabilityComparisons.operatorImpact: Choose Claude Code when hook-native workflows are central; use Codex when shared runtime and maintenance are the priority.

practicalImplications: Dual install is useful for teams that actively use both targets.
Codex-only is viable when minimal bootstrap and maintenance are more important than hook-native automation.

recommendedUsagePatterns.label: Dual install
whenToChoose: Need one shared runtime with two bridges
